channel.title: My DSA Journey — Weekly Digest
channel.description: Weekly rotation of one real-world case study, one pattern drill, and one hard-mode challenge from the 30-week DSA Journey.

item.title: Weekly Digest 2026-W28
item.description: Case study: Token Bucket vs Sliding Window at Cloudflare/AWS Pattern drill (Week 9, #9): Sort an array containing only 0/1 values in place, single pass, O(1) extra space. Challenge 1 (Week 9): Count Inversions Using Merge Sort

item.title: Weekly Digest 2026-W27
item.description: Case study: LRU vs LFU vs ARC in Redis Pattern drill (Week 8, #8): Find the integer square root of n (largest `k` with `k*k ≤ n`), n up to 10^18. Challenge 4 (Week 8): Aggressive Cows / Maximum Minimum Distance

item.title: Weekly Digest 2026-W26
item.description: Case study: How Ranking Interacts with Sorting + Priority Queues Pattern drill (Week 7, #7): Distractor: Given two strings `s` and `t` of length up to 10^6, decide whether `t` occurs in `s`. (Naïve is too slow — what now?) Challenge 3 (Week 7): KMP With Multiple Patterns at Once

item.title: Weekly Digest 2026-W25
item.description: Case study: Git's Commit Graph as a DAG, Why Topological Sort Matters Pattern drill (Week 6, #6): Given an array, move all zeros to the end, preserving the relative order of non-zero elements. In place. Challenge 2 (Week 6): In-Place Multi-Block Rotation

item.title: Weekly Digest 2026-W24
item.description: Case study: How Priority Queues Power a Real-Time Feed Pattern drill (Week 5, #5): Compute `gcd(a, b)` for positive integers a, b ≤ 10^9. Challenge 2 (Week 5): Mutual Recursion: Hofstadter Female/Male

item.title: Weekly Digest 2026-W23
item.description: Case study: Caching Strategies in DNS Resolvers Pattern drill (Week 4, #4): Print Pascal's triangle up to row `n` (n ≤ 20), each row centered. Challenge 1 (Week 4): Hollow Diamond With Border

item.title: Weekly Digest 2026-W22
item.description: Case study: Why Your CDN Uses Consistent Hashing (and What Happens Without It) Pattern drill (Week 3, #3): Given an integer `n`, determine whether it is a power of two. n fits in 32 bits. Challenge 3 (Week 3): Generalized Fibonacci (Pisano Period)

item.title: Weekly Digest 2026-W21
item.description: Case study: How "Discover Weekly" is a Graph + Matrix Factorization Problem Pattern drill (Week 2, #2): Read an integer score 0–100 and print the letter grade: A (>=90), B (>=80), C (>=70), D (>=60), F otherwise. Challenge 2 (Week 2): Tax Bracket Salary Computer
